questions.questionText: Autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is characterized by:
questions.optionB: Persistent deficits in social communication/interaction AND restricted, repetitive patterns of behavior/interests/activities — onset in early developmental period
questions.explanation: ASD (DSM-5): two core domains: (1) Persistent deficits in social communication/interaction (reduced social-emotional reciprocity, nonverbal communication deficits, difficulty developing/maintaining relationships). (2) Restricted, repetitive behaviors/interests/activities (stereotyped movements, insistence on sameness, restricted fixated interests, hyper/hyposensitivity to sensory input). Onset: early developmental period (often recognized by 2-3 years). Severity: Level 1 ('requiring support') to Level 3 ('requiring very substantial support'). Screening: M-CHAT at 18 and 24 months. Treatment: early behavioral intervention (ABA — applied behavior analysis), speech therapy, social skills training. Reference: Niraj Ahuja — ASD.
